relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: On the eighth day, priestly service begins before elders and people.
Aaron brings offerings for himself before serving others.
The people bring their offerings near.
Peace/well-being and grain offerings prepare for YHWH’s appearing.
The congregation stands before YHWH at the tent.
Moses says the commanded work will open to glory.
Aaron draws near to make covering for himself and the people.
Aaron begins with his own sin/misalignment offering.
Blood/life marks altar horns and base.
The inward portions rise as commanded.
Flesh and skin are burned outside camp.
The burnt offering’s blood/life circles the altar.
The pieces rise on the altar.
Washed inward parts and legs rise with the offering.
Aaron brings the people’s repair offering.
The burnt offering is made according to judgment.
The grain offering rises with the morning offering.
Peace/well-being offerings are slaughtered for the people.
The rich portions are prepared.
The fat portions rise on the altar.
Breasts and right thigh are waved before YHWH.
Aaron lifts his hands and blesses the people.
Moses and Aaron bless, and YHWH’s glory appears to all.
Fire comes from before YHWH, the people shout and fall on their faces.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 9 is the first priestly service flowering into glory: covering, offering, blessing, fire, and awe before the Presence.
